news.headline: Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP urges NMRA investors with losses to reach out after class action lawsuit.Chars: 103
news.summary: In Radnor, Pennsylvania, on February 19, 2025, the law firm Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP shared that a securities class action lawsuit has been initiated against Neumora Therapeutics, Inc. The lawsuit targets investors who bought or engaged with Neumora (NASDAQ: NMRA). For more information, visit www.ktmc.com.

news.headline: Cryptocurrencies rise post Trump's support. Bitcoin at $96,689.24 (+1.58%), Ethereum $2,716.15 (+2.13%), Dogecoin $0.2546 (+1.90%). Shorts exit market, liquidations at $132.69M. Long/Short ratio favors upside bets. Market sentiment from "Fear" to "Neutral."
news.summary: President Donald Trump's reaffirmation to make the U.S. the "cryptocurrency capital" boosted leading cryptocurrencies on Wednesday. Bitcoin's value increased by 1.58% to $96,689.24, Ethereum rose by 2.13% to $2,716.15, and Dogecoin saw a 1.90% gain to $0.2546. Bitcoin had over 60% dominance in the market, with Ethereum holding a 10% market share. Cryptocurrency liquidations totaled $132.69 million in the last 24 hours, wiping out $75 million in bearish bets. Bitcoin's Open Interest dropped by 0.28%, indicating shorts were closing positions. The Long/Short ratio for Bitcoin exceeded 1, suggesting a majority of traders were betting on its rise. Market sentiment shifted from "Fear" to "Neutral" following Trump's comments at a Miami conference.

news.headline: boj gov ueda enters Japan prime minister's residential office to meet Ishiba, jiji:
Codelco, Anglo American near partnership announcement with Tercera
OLED Association emphasizes regional competition boosts display industry; tracks tech evolution from CRTs to Micro LEDs.
news.summary: A recent study conducted in LA JOLLA, Calif. by the OLED Association examined the evolution of display technology and production sources. The research traced advancements from CRTs to LCDs, Plasma, OLEDs, and currently, Micro LEDs. This progress has been ongoing since the mid-20th century. The study sheds light on the factors contributing to the success of the display industry, showcasing the rapid growth and transformation within the sector.

news.headline: Ericsson and Telstra partner for first programmable network in Asia-Pacific, a 4-year deployment to enhance connectivity capabilities for customers, driving innovation and new monetization opportunities.
news.summary: Telstra is planning a four-year deployment that will enhance its network and related services, positioning it as a leader on the global stage. The improved network capabilities will provide advanced connectivity for Telstra's consumer and enterprise clients, paving the way for new revenue streams. This initiative is expected to significantly boost innovation across various sectors.

news.headline: China's Zhu: Firms following the law don't need to worry about investigations

Lundin Mining reported Q4 2024 results highlighted by record copper production and revenue of $1.02 billion. Full-year revenue was $4.1 billion with 369,067 tonnes of copper produced. Net debt was $1.33 billion, expected to reduce with asset sales. Operations in 2025 include copper production of 303,000-330,000 tonnes with cash costs of $2.05-$2.30/lb. Company also outlined capital expenditure guidance.
news.summary: Lundin Mining Corporation reported its financial results for Q4 and full year 2024, highlighting transformative transactions and record copper/zinc production. They formed Vicuña Corp. aiming to become a top-tier copper producer. Revenue in Q4 was $1,023.8 million, with $870 million annual free cash flow, and net debt of approx $1.3 billion. Full year 2024 saw revenue of $4,117 million, 369,067 tonnes of copper production, and adjusted earnings of $358.9 million. Operational highlights included copper, zinc, and gold production. Cash and cash equivalents were $357.5 million. The company announced guidance for 2025, including production, cash costs, and capital expenditures. Lundin Mining focused on growth opportunities and asset performance while balancing financial stability.

4th US state, Montana, pushes Bitcoin reserve bill to House after subcommittee approval, joining three others.
news.summary: A Bitcoin reserve bill has been approved at the subcommittee level in Montana, marking it as the fourth state in the US to progress such legislation to the House. This move signifies a growing trend towards digital currency integration at the state level, with a growing number of states considering similar bills. The approval in Montana demonstrates the increasing acceptance and recognition of Bitcoin as a legitimate financial asset.

Global refractories market projected to reach $42.4 billion by 2029, growing at a CAGR of 4.4%, driven by factors like industrialization and technological advancements. Key players include RHI Magnesita, Vesuvius, Krosaki Harima Corporation, Imerys, and others. Acidic & Neutral segment expected to have highest growth, with Iron & Steel segment dominating in 2023. Shaped refractories segment is the fastest growing. China's monopoly over raw materials poses a restraint. Recycling of refractories presents an opportunity.
news.summary: The global refractories market is projected to grow from USD 34.3 billion in 2024 to USD 42.4 billion by 2029, at a CAGR of 4.4% during the forecast period. Refractories are specialized materials designed to withstand high temperatures and harsh industrial conditions, used in steelmaking, glass production, cement manufacturing, and chemical processing. Key factors driving market growth include increasing industrialization in sectors like steel, cement, and chemicals, infrastructure development projects boosting demand for construction materials, and advancements in refractory technology improving performance and lifespan. The market is segmented into various forms such as bricks, castables, and monolithic formulations. Challenges include China's monopoly over raw materials and fluctuations in raw material prices. Key players in the market include RHI Magnesita, Vesuvius, Krosaki Harima Corporation, Imerys, Shinagawa Refractories Co., Saint-Gobain, and Calderys. The Acidic & Neutral segment is expected to witness the highest growth, with the Iron & Steel segment dominating the market in 2023 and the Shaped refractories segment being the fastest-growing segment.

'Fantasy Top' game, utilizing Crypto Twitter (X), expands to Monad following testnet launch on Ethereum L2 Blast.
news.summary: Fantasy Top has transformed Crypto Twitter into a fantasy sports game called X, with a successful launch on Ethereum L2 Blast. The game is now expanding to Monad, with a large number of users participating in the platform.

Trump Addresses Reporters Aboard Air Force One
Law firm KTMC reminds ICLR investors to contact firm for loss claims on ICLR securities class action lawsuit.
news.summary: In Radnor, Pennsylvania on February 19, 2025, Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP announced a securities class action lawsuit against ICON Public Limited Company (NASDAQ: ICLR). The lawsuit has been filed on behalf of all purchasers of ICON ordinary shares. For more information, investors can visit the law firm's website at www.ktmc.com.

news.headline: New quantum chip by Microsoft harnesses topological superconductivity to scale quantum computing with 1 million qubits.
news.summary: The Majorana 1 processor by Microsoft utilizes topological superconductivity to potentially boost quantum computing to a scale of 1 million qubits.

news.headline: Chorus Aviation Inc. reports $607.7M net proceeds from RAL sale, $211.6M adjusted EBITDA for 2024; Share Consolidation completed.
news.summary: Chorus Aviation Inc. reported its financial results for the fourth quarter and year-end 2024, highlighting the completion of the sale of the RAL business with net proceeds of US $607.7 million. The leverage ratio improved to 1.4 at the end of 2024 from 3.3 in 2023. The company had a net loss from continuing operations of $15.8 million and adjusted earnings available to common shareholders of $28.5 million. Adjusted EBITDA was $211.6 million, with free cash flow amounting to $118.8 million. In the fourth quarter, the net loss was $6.6 million, with adjusted earnings available to common shareholders of $10.6 million. Share consolidation occurred on February 5, 2025, with Chorus consolidating its common shares. The company emphasized the strengthening of its balance sheet and focus on growing its aviation services business post the RAL sale.

news.headline: Tashi Baijiu wins #1 White Spirit at San Diego Spirits Challenge, using traditional Tibetan methods.
news.summary: In San Diego on February 19, 2025, Tashi Baijiu "The Spirit of Tibet" was named the top White Spirit at the San Diego International Spirits Challenge (SDISC). This premium artisanal white spirit, crafted using traditional Tibetan techniques, received the prestigious accolade. The recognition solidifies Tashi Baijiu's position as the leading white spirit at the event.

news.headline: Sitochu forms capital, business alliance with Technologent
Technologent partners with ITOCHU, aiming to boost innovation and growth in IT solutions for diverse industries.
news.summary: In Irvine, California, a recent partnership has been formed between Technologent and ITOCHU to boost innovation and growth in IT solutions across various industries. The collaboration will capitalize on cutting-edge technologies to drive progress in the enterprise sector.

news.headline: Toshiba introduces small photorelays in Kawasaki, Japan for semiconductor testers, with high speed turn-on time.
news.summary: A recent development in Kawasaki, Japan involves Toshiba's introduction of compact photorelays in a small package. These new products are designed to provide a high-speed turn-on time, aiding in the reduction of test duration for semiconductor testers. The innovation aims to enhance efficiency and streamline processes in the semiconductor industry, offering significant benefits in terms of speed and compact design.

news.headline: Citi India looking to purchase $1 billion in asset-backed securities.
dYdX Foundation CEO predicts DeFi to surge in September with increased accessibility for new users.
news.summary: The CEO of the dYdX Foundation, Charles d’Haussy, anticipates a significant surge in the DeFi sector come September. He foresees an increase in entry points for newcomers to this decentralized finance space, suggesting that more opportunities will be available for new users to participate in DeFi activities.

news.headline: Oil maintains increase as attention shifts to US stockpiles and supply uncertainty.
Trump aims to make US the 'crypto capital', mentioning record highs in Bitcoin post-election. Reiterates commitment at conference.
news.summary: During a conference in Miami, President Donald Trump reaffirmed his administration's goal to establish the U.S. as the leading global hub for cryptocurrency on Wednesday. Trump highlighted Bitcoin's (CRYPTO: BTC) historic highs following his election win, emphasizing his commitment to elevating America as the epicenter of crypto. "Bitcoin has reached numerous peak values because the nation recognizes my dedication to positioning America as the dominant force in the cryptocurrency realm," Trump declared. He expressed the desire for the U.S. to lead in various spheres, including the realm of cryptocurrency. The president's assertions were made during the Future Investment Initiative Institute conference in Miami, underlining his determination to position the U.S. at the forefront of the cryptocurrency industry.

news.headline: Taseko reports $224M Adjusted EBITDA in 2024, with $608M Revenue from 108M lbs copper sales and 1.4M lbs molybdenum. Q4 saw $56M EBITDA and 29M lbs copper production, with a plan to increase copper production in 2025 to 120-130M lbs, and construction at Florence Copper progressing towards first copper production by Q4 2025. The company maintains a strong financial position with a cash balance of $173M and $331M available liquidity, along with copper price protection at US$4.00/lb for 2025. Acquisition of remaining interest in Gibraltar occurred in Q1 2024. Promising outlook with continued growth opportunities.
news.summary: Taseko Mines Limited reported a full year 2024 Adjusted EBITDA of $224 million and revenues of $608 million from selling 108 million pounds of copper and 1.4 million pounds of molybdenum. They had a net loss of $13 million and adjusted net income of $57 million for the year. In the fourth quarter, they had an Adjusted EBITDA of $56 million, earnings from mining operations of $59 million, and a net loss of $21 million. Gibraltar Mine produced 29 million pounds of copper and 578 thousand pounds of molybdenum in the fourth quarter. Copper production in 2025 is expected to be 120 to 130 million pounds. Additionally, the Florence Copper project is progressing on schedule with first copper production expected in the fourth quarter of 2025. The company had a year-end cash balance of $173 million and available liquidity of approximately $331 million. 2024 results showed that Gibraltar mine produced 105.6 million pounds of copper and 1.4 million pounds of molybdenum. Total operating costs for the year were $2.66 per pound produced. The Company also completed an offering of US$500 million Senior Secured Notes due 2030.

news.headline: Spartan Delta Corp. announces 2024 results: 38,166 BOE/d production, $301.6 million sales, $164.6 million AFF, 250,000 Duvernay acres.
news.summary: Spartan Delta Corp. reported their financial and operational results for Q4 and the year ended December 31, 2024. Highlights include production of 38,166 BOE/d in 2024, with oil and gas sales of $301.6 million and Adjusted Funds Flow of $164.6 million. The Company executed a capital program of $161.9 million. They achieved a 255% increase in crude oil production and a 16% increase in condensate production in Q4 2024 compared to Q4 2023. Spartan generated Free Funds Flow of $2.7 million in 2024 and exited 2024 with Net Debt of $148.1 million. They increased their Duvernay production to over 5,000 BOE/d. PDP reserves decreased by 1%, while TP reserves increased by 7%, and TPP reserves increased by 7% in 2024. Spartan completed an equity financing for gross proceeds of approximately $97.8 million. This resulted in key numbers showing a decrease in net income and comprehensive income from $110,584 in 2023 to $5,189 in 2024, generating a moderate decrease in oil and gas sales from $652,769 in 2023 to $301,640 in 2024, among other financial changes documented in the provided data.

news.headline: Boxlight announces $2.8M private placement, selling 1,323,000 shares and warrants, priced under Nasdaq rules.
news.summary: Boxlight Corporation, a prominent player in educational technology, recently revealed its latest financial move. The company informed that it has engaged in securities purchase agreements for the acquisition and sale of 1,323,000 shares of its Class A common stock or Common Stock equivalents alternatively. Additionally, as part of the private placement, the company also issued warrants enabling the purchase of up to 1,323,000 shares of Common Stock. This private placement was priced at-the-market under Nasdaq guidelines.

Calls for Trump to stop firing FAA employees following fatal plane crashes, amid concerns over transportation department cuts.
news.summary: Following a series of tragic airplane accidents, legislators and labor representatives are imploring the president to safeguard the transportation department from additional layoffs that could jeopardize safety.
